title: Smoky Gooey Australian Chicken Roast
description: A deliciously smoky and gooey roast chicken packed with blueberries, perfect for a post‑workout meal that’s also FODMAP‑friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F) and line a roasting pan with parchment paper.
In a small bowl whisk together olive oil, smoked paprika, sea salt, black pepper, dried rosemary, garlic‑infused oil, lemon zest, and maple syrup.
Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels and rub them all over with the spice mixture, skin side up in the pan.
Scatter the blueberries evenly around the chicken pieces.
Pour the low‑FODMAP chicken broth into the pan and add the remaining blueberries to create a flavorful base.
Roast for 30 minutes, then carefully flip each thigh and continue roasting for another 15 minutes, or until the skin is crisp and the internal temperature reaches 75°C (165°F).
Remove the chicken from the oven and tent loosely with foil, allowing it to rest for 5 minutes.
While the chicken rests, pour the pan juices into a small saucepan and reduce over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Whisk in the unsalted butter to create a silky reduction sauce; stir until the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Return the chicken to the pan, spoon the reduction over the pieces, and garnish with a sprinkle of fresh rosemary before serving.
